text:
Once upon a time, in a quaint little town encircled by towering redwoods, lived a dog named Max. Max didn’t just bark. He had an inner monologue that matched his human’s introspection. Each evening as the sun set behind the dense forest, he’d muse about life’s little mysteries.

One particular morning, Max woke to a peculiar noise. It was a sort of chittering, higher pitched than anything he’d ever heard. He twitched his ears, sensing curiosity. Curiosity being one of his top instincts.

That afternoon, Max decided to investigate what that chittering could be. It wasn’t hard to find; all he had to do was listen intently. What he discovered was a community of squirrels organizing a symposium about how best to hoard acorns for the winter.

Max listened fascinated. They debated theories on the best location for hoarding, lamented over shortages, and had debates whether it was better to eat them raw or
